description: Following Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Germany moved to accelerate its renewable energy transition to decouple from Russian gas. The policy shift aimed for 80% renewable electricity by 2030, sparking intense debate on energy security versus environmental goals.

key_events.description: Russia invades Ukraine, triggering immediate energy crisis awareness.
German government introduces the 'Easter Package' legislation for 100% renewable power target.
Gas prices peak, solidifying public demand for energy independence through renewables.

calibration_notes: Measures how geopolitical urgency can shift consensus on long-term environmental infrastructure.
